source: WI-09 blind-definer (fresh context, spec-only, register withheld)

terms.label: Memo
terms.definition: A versioned, local strategy document (living in a {NNN}-{slug} directory under an un-versioned .memo/ tree) that is the single highest authority over its own rollout, iterating through revisions until finalized and then driving that rollout to produce real artifacts.
terms.notMeaning: It is not a cross-memo intent that outlives it (that is a Goal) and not an uploadable/public document — the spec fixes it as local and single-scoped.

terms.label: Revision
terms.definition: One immutable REV-XX.md snapshot of a memo's content — Full (standalone) or Update (delta on the preceding Full) — where every change produces a new forward-only file.
terms.notMeaning: It is not an in-place edit of a single growing file; the spec forbids mutating a revision.

terms.label: Topic
terms.definition: An atomic, memo-scoped point extracted from the input during the five-step pipeline, forming a complete checklist and the head of the Topic -> Block -> PRD chain.
terms.notMeaning: It is not a work item (the finding-level unit below it) and not a block; a topic is the user's view.

terms.label: Goal
terms.definition: A cross-memo intent (id G###) that sits above any single memo, spans several memos, and is scored in a fresh context against real state on a fluid offen<->abgeschlossen lifecycle.
terms.notMeaning: It is not a surface label but the intent behind it, and not the chronicle (which narrates without grading).

terms.label: Block
terms.definition: An addressable structure node inside a memo (id B###) that gathers one or more topics via topicIds, holds four sections (factual account, assessment, solution, open questions), and whose tags feed the auto-requirement scope match.
terms.notMeaning: It is explicitly not a 'context block' and carries no strand field.

terms.label: PRD
terms.definition: A block-derived chunk of work sized so one PRD fits into one context (hard cap 1/3, smart-zone 1/4), implementable by one agent in one fresh empty thread, declaring its Required-Context as a pointer table.
terms.notMeaning: It is not a whole feature and is never split across more than one thread — a second thread is the signal to chunk into more PRDs.

terms.label: Phase
terms.definition: A sequential, mandatory rollout unit that bundles PRDs to be executed together and in order, carries an orchestration role (Lead/Worker/Evaluator), and must declare its depends-on (and symmetric can-parallel-with).
terms.notMeaning: It is not merely an unordered batch of PRDs — depends-on is a mandatory characteristic.

terms.label: Strand
terms.definition: The emergent dependency closure over phases — the transitive closure of the depends-on edges in ## Phase-Hints — derived by walking the graph and carrying its PRDs.
terms.notMeaning: It is not a thematic bundle the author hand-picks and not part of the numeric memo ID; it is computed, not authored (a tag).

terms.label: Requirement
terms.definition: A single addressable statement that must/should/may hold, stored one file per entry under .memo/_requirements/, two-sided (statement faces generation, check faces the gate) with an optional grade axis and ternary PASS/BLOCKED/INCONCLUSIVE status.
terms.notMeaning: It is not a structural link in the Topic->Block->PRD chain but a cross-cutting check; grading is a scoring head on it, not a twelfth primitive.

terms.label: Tool
terms.definition: An external capability a phase or PRD depends on, recorded as a short reference descriptor in the per-project tools registry under .memo/tools/ so reachability is a planning-time concern.
terms.notMeaning: The registry is descriptive, not a runtime, and only a RECOMMENDATION — it must not embed copies of skills/source, and its absence must not block a memo.

terms.label: Plan
terms.definition: The execution-side primitive above the memo (id PLAN-###-slug) that spans one or more finalized memos and sequences their phases into a single ordered run, with state in plan-status.json.
terms.notMeaning: It does not replace the per-memo four-stage model — it nests it — and differs from a Goal (which spans memos on the intent side).
